# coding-max maintenance suite v0.0.5beta

This Beta turns `coding-max` into a bounded incident runtime with auditable case reuse and an evaluator-isolated adversarial benchmark, while keeping the suite focused on brownfield maintenance.

## What changed

- Added orthogonal lifecycle and diagnostic stages instead of scattering state across repair prose.
- Added evidence-based Actionability and Human Gates for permanent repairs, public contracts, security, data, and irreversible choices.
- Made historical-case retrieval execute between failure confirmation and localization, with `matched`, `no-match`, and `unavailable` results written back to the active case.
- Added conservative compatibility for legacy case indexes without allowing historical similarity to prove the current cause.
- Added three isolated adversarial cases for misleading timeouts, unrelated dirty baselines, and functionally green but degraded repairs.
- Added evaluator-only ground truth, host-captured manifests, digest evidence, fatal integrity failures, and weighted scoring anchors.
- Removed Codacy and Dependency Review jobs whose unavailable repository prerequisites produced permanent non-actionable failures.

## Evidence

- 23 contract and integration tests cover the four Skills, incident runtime, case feedback loop, context budgets, installation, and release consistency.
- Three adversarial source packages validate with public inputs separated from evaluator-only truth.
- GitHub checks cover the Skill contracts, CodeQL for Python and JavaScript, and DevSkim.
- The four Skills remain independently discoverable, host-neutral, single-agent, and progressively disclosed.

The benchmark infrastructure is executable, but retained cross-model run artifacts and aggregate success rates are not claimed. Local project Bug, Review, Pipeline, Tombstone, and profile records are not included in the release.
